input.prompt:
I'm building a C# MCP server using stdio transport. I need to add a tool
that calls an external API using HttpClient (injected via DI), and a prompt
template for summarization. Make sure logging doesn't interfere with the
stdio JSON-RPC protocol.

data.content:
I'm building a C# MCP server using stdio transport. I need to add a tool
that calls an external API using HttpClient (injected via DI), and a prompt
template for summarization. Make sure logging doesn't interfere with the
stdio JSON-RPC protocol.

<current_datetime>2026-05-13T00:54:07.345Z</current_datetime>

I'm building a C# MCP server using stdio transport. I need to add a tool
that calls an external API using HttpClient (injected via DI), and a prompt
template for summarization. Make sure logging doesn't interfere with the
stdio JSON-RPC protocol.

input.initialPrompt:
I'm building a C# MCP server using stdio transport. I need to add a tool
that calls an external API using HttpClient (injected via DI), and a prompt
template for summarization. Make sure logging doesn't interfere with the
stdio JSON-RPC protocol.

arguments.command: dotnet --version 2>/dev/null && dotnet new list --tag console 2>/dev/null | head -5

data.reasoningText:
The user wants to build a C# MCP (Model Context Protocol) server using stdio transport. They need:

1. A tool that calls an external API using HttpClient (injected via DI)
2. A prompt template for summarization
3. Proper logging that doesn't interfere with stdio JSON-RPC protocol

Let me create a complete C# project for this. I'll need to:

1. Create a new C# project
2. Add the MCP SDK NuGet package
3. Implement the tool with HttpClient DI
4. Implement the prompt template
5. Configure logging to stderr (not stdout, since stdout is used for JSON-RPC)


I'll start by setting up the project structure and dependencies.

arguments.command: dotnet --version 2>/dev/null && dotnet new list --tag console 2>/dev/null | head -5

data.content: Good, packages installed. Now let me check the MCP SDK's API surface to use the correct types:

toolCalls.args: {"command": "grep -E '<member name=\"[TMP]\\.' ~/.nuget/packages/modelcontextprotocol/1.3.0/lib/net9.0/ModelContextProtocol.xml | grep -iE '(tool|prompt|server|stdio|attribute|McpServerTool|McpServerPrompt|WithStdio|AddMcpServer)' | head -40", "description": "Explore MCP SDK API surface", "initial_wait": 10}
